Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Resposnes To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-26-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Christopher J. Olson, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Ken Lim & Company
Daniel L. Mitchell, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Benjamin Throne

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted as modified. Defendant Kent Lim & Company's motion to compel further responses to request for production of documents set one; and request for monetary sanctions:
a. As to request for production no. 17, granted as to any documents, not privileged, which tend to quantify or specify the amount, nature or extent of plaintiff's alleged damages;
b. As to request for production nos. 19 and 20, denied without prejudice to propounding redrafted requests for production; the requests are, inter alia, overbroad;
c. As to request for production nos. 22, 23 and 24, denied without prejudice to propounding redrafted requests for production; the requests are, inter alia, vague and ambiguous;
d. As to request for production nos. 35, Granted, subject to attorney clien and attorney work product privilege
e. As to request for production nos. 36, Granted, subject to attorney clien and attorney work product privilege
f. As to request for production nos. 37 and 38, Granted, subject to attorney clien and attorney work product privilege
g. As to request for production nos. 39, granted; plaintiff shall serve a verified supplemental response within ten days of the date of notice of entry of this order;
h. As to request for production nos. 43 through 69, inclusive, and No. 72, denied without prejudice to propounding redrafted requests for production; the request is, inter alia, overbroad;
i. Request for sanctions denied. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Resposnes To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, And Request For Monetary Sanctions

Notice And Motion For 1. An Issue And Evidence Sanctions Against Deft Kent M. Lim & Co. 2. Monetary Sanctions Against Deft For Disorbedience Of The Orders Of The Court Dated March 23,2009
Off Calendar Jun-16-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance, tentative ruling is adopted.
1. Motion for issue and evidence sanctions against defendant Kent M. Lim & Company denied without prejudice;
2. Within five days of the date of service of this order--and in compliance with the court's order of March 23, 2009--defendant shall serve verified supplemental responses to plaintiff's form interrogatories (employment) nos. 200.1, 200.3. and 200.4, to provide further required identifying information (including address and telephone number) for any individuals named in defendant's previous responses; and nos. 201.1, 201.3, 204.5, and 204.7 (including code compliant responses to each subpart, and a more specific response to plaintiff's form interrogatory (employment) no. 204.5
3. Motion for monetary sanctions against defendant Kent M. Lim & Company and Christopher J. Olson, its attorney, for willful disobedience of the order of the court dated March 23, 2009, granted; sanctions shall issue in the sum of $790.00, to run against defendant Kent M. Lim & Company, and its attorney of record. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.
